channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump warns Iran of further strikes as ceasefire ends in Middle East tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has warned Iran that the United States is preparing for additional strikes following the collapse of a ceasefire agreement. The warning came after Iranian attacks on American mi...

item.title: Carbon Monoxide Risks from Indoor Imbawula Use: Safety Precautions Needed
item.description: As temperatures drop in South Africa, the use of imbawulas (coal braziers) for heating and cooking has increased. However, emergency services are cautioning against indoor use due to carbon monoxide p...

item.title: Kenyan PhD Candidate Successfully Defends Dissertation on Food Insecurity
item.description: Michael Atuahene Djan, a Kenyan PhD candidate, recently successfully defended his dissertation on food insecurity among college students in Nebraska. The academic journey, which he once described as f...

item.title: Suspended Legal Head Arrested in Disciplinary Case Involving Alleged Corruption
item.description: Adv. Kemi Behari, the suspended head of legal services in Ekurhuleni, has been arrested by the Political Killings Task Team (PKTT) following a disciplinary hearing. This development marks progress in ...

item.title: Abuja Police Arrest Kidnapping Gang, Recover ₦8.8m Ransom
item.description: FCT Police have successfully arrested a notorious 'one chance' kidnapping gang in Abuja after recovering ₦8.8 million ransom, two vehicles, and other stolen items. The gang targeted a female victim, d...

item.title: Toronto's World Cup saw increased use of walking, cycling, and transit
item.description: Data from the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Toronto reveals a significant shift in urban mobility patterns. During the tournament, pedestrian activity around the Toronto Stadium surged by 130% on the first m...

item.title: Amit Shah to Lead Border Security Conference on Migration and Demographics
item.description: The article discusses the upcoming conference chaired by Gujarat Chief Minister Amit Shah, focusing on border security challenges. The event will bring together senior police officers (SPs) to address...

item.title: Tony Elumelu Retires as UBA Group Chairman After 12 Years, Emmanuel Nnorom to Take Over
item.description: Tony Elumelu, the billionaire businessman and philanthropist, is stepping down as Chairman of United Bank for Africa (UBA) after a 12-year tenure. His retirement date is set for August 21, 2026, follo...

item.title: Environmental Concerns Rise as Illegal Mining Increases Silt in Lake Rukwa
item.description: Unregulated mining activities in Tanzania's Rukwa region are causing severe sedimentation in Lake Rukwa, threatening its ecosystem and local communities. The extraction of minerals has led to soil ero...

item.title: Rescue operations ongoing after typhoon hits China amid flood warnings
item.description: Rescue teams are actively searching for survivors in regions affected by recent storms in China as a powerful super typhoon approaches. The situation has raised concerns about potential flooding and d...

item.title: Diplomatic Feud Escalates as Trump and Meloni Clash at NATO Summit
item.description: President Donald Trump has intensified diplomatic tensions with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni following a heated exchange at a NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey. During a traditional family photo ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy locates wreckage of missing cargo plane near Ormara coast
item.description: The Pakistan Navy and Maritime Security Agency (PMSA) have located the wreckage of a missing cargo aircraft approximately 53 nautical miles south of Ormara coast. The aircraft, operated by a private c...

item.title: IMF projects 3% global growth for 2026 despite Iran war impacts, with AI aiding recovery
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has revised its outlook for global economic growth to 3% in 2026, down from previous forecasts due to the energy crisis caused by the Iran conflict. However, inve...

item.title: Defense Justifies Duterte's Threats Against Marcos in Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continues as her defense team argues that her alleged threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. were justified due to perceived threats agains...

item.title: Legal battle erupts over 2027 Comrades Marathon organisation
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Athletics (KZNA) has issued a legal threat against the Comrades Marathon Association (CMA) over the organisation of the 2027 Comrades Marathon, claiming the event has not been sanctioned...

item.title: Four Individuals Deported Back to India After Being Forced into Bangladesh
item.description: Four individuals from West Bengal's Birbhum district, who were allegedly forced into Bangladesh in June 2025, were finally brought back to India after a prolonged legal battle. The group, including Su...

item.title: EU's Digital Border System (EES) Faces Operational Challenges at Airports
item.description: The European Union's Digital Border System (EES), designed to streamline cross-border travel and enhance security, has encountered significant operational challenges at airports across member states. ...

item.title: Government Payroll Scandal Under Investigation: DCI Probes KSh 6.2 Billion Fraud
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has launched a probe into a suspected KSh 6.2 billion payroll fraud after receiving a special audit report highlighting widespread irregularities in go...

item.title: Tanzania's 50th Dar es Salaam Trade Fair Aims for Global Hub Status by 2050
item.description: The 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) 2026 officially opened on July 3, 2026, with President Samia Suluhu Hassan and Mozambique’s President Daniel Chapo inaugurating the event. The fa...

item.title: Poll suggests broad Canadian support for most Alberta referendum proposals, though experts question the wording
item.description: A new Leger poll commissioned by the Association for Canadian Studies suggests that many Canadians, both inside and outside Alberta, support most of the province's upcoming referendum questions that f...
